aids.title: Complexity Memory Trick
aids.content:
**L**inear = **L**ength of array = O(**n**)

'Linear' and 'Length' both start with L, and complexity equals the Length (n)

aids.title: SCAN
aids.content:
**S**tart at beginning
**C**ompare with target
**A**dvance to next
**N**ot found? Return -1

aids.title: The Library Analogy
aids.content: Imagine searching for a book in an **unsorted library**. You have no choice but to check each book one by one from the first shelf. That's linear search - simple but potentially slow for large libraries.

aids.title: Linear vs Binary Quick Check
aids.content:
**Unsorted? Use Linear.** **Sorted? Use Binary.**

Linear = O(n) for any data
Binary = O(log n) but needs sorted data
aids.forRemembering: Choosing between linear and binary search

aids.title: The Pointer Walk
aids.content:
Visualize a single pointer walking through an array left to right, stopping to check each element: [x] -> [x] -> [FOUND!]

One direction, one element at a time, one pointer.

aids.title: Complexity Rhyme
aids.content:
Linear search, one by one,
O(n) time till it's done.
Space is constant, just O(1),
Simple search for everyone!
